visual_attributes.morphology: Triangular glow extending from the Sun along the zodiac
distinctive_features: Appears as a column tilted at the angle of the ecliptic
visual_attributes.size_and_scale: Spans the entire sky, visible in a dark night sky
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: Lens-shaped volume centered on the Sun

functional_attributes.primary_function: Scattering of sunlight by interplanetary dust
secondary_functions: Contributes to natural light of the night sky

domain_specific_functional.DUST_ORIGIN: Primarily from Jupiter-family comets and asteroid collisions
domain_specific_functional.ECLIPTIC_VISIBILITY: Best observed near the horizon at small angles with the Sun

relationships.description: Both are phenomena caused by sunlight scattering off interplanetary dust
Dust from these comets contributes to the zodiacal light

contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: Referenced in Islamic tradition as 'false dawn'
primary_sources: Historical records by Giovanni Domenico Cassini
